title: GeoToSchemtic
description: Convert KML and GeoJSON files to Minecraft schematics for BuildTheEarth project!
description2:
Upload your files and set the desired settings, then click "Convert".
Use //paste -o -a command to paste your converted schematic into the correct position in BTE projection.
status_success: Success! Use "//paste -a -o" to correctly place your schematic
status_error: Error while reading the file. Please make sure that your file is correct
status_error_toobig: Hold on, your file is too big for one schematic! How about splitting it into smaller ones?
status_error_wronglegacyid: Wrong block ID, please use text IDs of blocks that are presented in 1.12.2
status_error_exceedminecraftlimit: The size of the proposed schematic exceeds the limits set by Minecraft
status_error_wrongcoordsformat: Your file contains coordinates in an unsupported format. The coordinates of each point must be expressed as latitude and longitude (and, optionally, altitude) as decimal degrees
originexplanation: These coordinates are needed for some cases, e.g. if you're using Litematica. With WorldEdit, just use //paste with "-o" flag. Pasting it without the flag will result in the schematic being inserted in an unexpected location in your world!
offset_tooltip:
Additional schematic offset.
Some BTE servers have an additional world offset. Enter this offset here, and it will be added to the schematic's original BTE-projection coordinates. Most servers don't have an additional offset, so check the "Offset presets".
conselevation_tooltip: Place objects at their expected height in the schematic based on their altitude value specified in the kml/geojson file.<hr>If unchecked, all objects will be on Y level 0, regardless of their altitude values. You can move them to another level using Y offset.
fillterrain_tooltip:
Fill gaps between contour lines in a topographic schematic. This is an experimental feature. It increases the overall processing time for large schematics. There may also be blocks that are placed 1 block lower than they should be, but they are easy to spot and fix in-game manually.
Emerald blocks will be generated between all diamond blocks along the X and Z axes (using scanline fill algorythm). The diamond contours themselves will remain intact. In order for this to work properly, there must be a vertical interval of 0-1 meter between contours, and they should not intersect.
makefoundation_tooltip: Creates a foundation under all blocks in the schematic. This brings convenience to terraforming, particularly when working with falling blocks.
privacynotice:
Privacy notice: all files are processed by your local machine
and aren't uploaded to any server.
error_wrongid: Please enter a valid Minecraft block ID. Only text IDs are supported
error_offset: Please enter correct offset values. Offset values can only be integers
alert_toobig:
The proposed schematic has a very large volume of blocks. Please note that pasting such a schematic may be prohibited on your BTE server without the administration's consent.
Click "OK" to continue the conversion; "Cancel" to cancel the conversion.
